run.initial_example:
Concept: Compound Interest

Example:
Amara, a nurse in Lagos, wants to grow ₦200,000 using compound interest. She finds a savings account offering 6% annual interest, compounded monthly.

Breakdown:
- Principal (P): ₦200,000
- Annual rate (r): 6% = 0.06
- Compounds per year (n): 12
- Time (t): 3 years

Formula:
A = P × (1 + r/n)^(n×t)

Plug in:
A = ₦200,000 × (1 + 0.06/12)^(12×3)
  = ₦200,000 × (1.005)^(36)

Approximate calculation:
(1.005)^(36) ≈ 1.1967
A ≈ ₦200,000 × 1.1967 ≈ ₦239,340

Interest earned ≈ A − P ≈ ₦39,340

Key Insight:
Compound interest grows faster the more often interest is added to the balance; monthly compounding at 6% for 3 years yields about ₦39,000 in interest on ₦200,000.

The analogy of growing savings through compound interest is domain-appropriate, using financial concepts relevant to the topic.
